boardTopology.intro: See how your printer's control boards connect: the host (SBC) and every MCU it talks to, with each connection type and a best-effort board / chip guess — read live from the config.

steps: The host (your SBC, e.g. a Raspberry Pi or CB1) sits at the top.
Each MCU card shows how it connects (USB / CAN bus / UART) and its chip.
A board guess appears when the connection identifier matches a known board.

views.title: Physical vs Logical views
views.body: The map draws the same boards two ways. PHYSICAL shows how the machine is actually built: an integrated SBC is drawn inside the mainboard it ships on, CAN toolheads hang off a shared backbone rail, and USB/UART boards are separate units. LOGICAL shows how Klipper sees it: the host at the top with every MCU as a child reached over its serial link. Toggle with the Physical/Logical switch.

mcus.title: MCUs
mcus.body: An MCU is a microcontroller board that drives motors, heaters and sensors. A printer has at least one (the mainboard); many add a toolhead board and sometimes a probe or LED board. This panel lists every `[mcu]` section Klipper has.

connections.title: Connection types
connections.body: USB — a serial-over-USB link (the usual mainboard connection). CAN bus — a shared two-wire bus, common for toolhead boards. UART — a direct serial link over GPIO. The type is inferred from each MCU's `serial` / `canbus_uuid` setting.

detection.title: How detection works
detection.body: The chip and board are guessed by matching the connection identifier (the serial path or CAN UUID) against a pattern table, plus a pin-map fingerprint of the pins your config uses. A board guess shows a match confidence; when nothing matches, the chip/board reads "unknown" — the connection itself is still shown.

override.title: Confirm or change a board
override.body: A serial id usually reveals only the chip, so the board guess can be missing or wrong. Use "Confirm this board" to lock in a correct guess, or "Change board…" / "Set board…" to pick the right one from the catalog. Your choice is saved on the host and reused on every read; "Clear" reverts to the automatic guess.

mcu.def: Microcontroller unit — a control board running Klipper's firmware (mainboard, toolhead, etc.).

canbus.def: A two-wire shared bus linking boards by a unique UUID — popular for toolhead wiring.

host.def: The single-board computer (Pi, CB1, …) that runs Klipper and talks to the MCUs.

graph.integratedNote: The host SBC is built into the {board} mainboard.
graph.selectPrompt: Select a board or MCU in the map to inspect it.
graph.aria: Machine map: the host and {n} MCUs

states.empty: No MCUs found in the config.

board.none: No catalog details for this board yet.
board.noMatch: No board matched yet — confirm or set one below.

override.hint: Your choice is saved on the host and used for this MCU from now on.
